title: Robust Spin Logic Enabled by Generalized $\mathrm{SU}(2)$ Symmetry in $p$-Wave Magnets
authors: Hao-Kun Ke, Gong Zhao, Siqing Li, Ruixiang Chen, Chui-Zhen Chen
abstract: Unconventional magnets combine the vanishing stray fields of antiferromagnets with the strong spin-splitting of ferromagnets, offering a unique material platform for spintronics. However, a critical challenge in realizing functional spin-logic devices lies in preserving long-range spin coherence against momentum-degrading scattering and gate-induced dephasing. Here, we demonstrate that the intrinsic momentum-dependent exchange field of a three-dimensional $p$-wave magnet can be precisely tuned against gate-induced Rashba spin-orbit coupling to establish a \textit{generalized} $\mathrm{SU}(2)$ spin-rotation symmetry. This emergent conservation law generates a symmetry-protected Persistent Spin Helix (PSH), effectively integrating the high energy scales of 3D bulk magnetic exchange with the macroscopic coherence of symmetry protection. By modeling a synergistic $p$-wave magnetic spin field-effect transistor (spin-FET), we reveal high-visibility Datta-Das conductance oscillations controlled purely by electrical gating. Crucially, our quantum transport simulations confirm that this symmetry-engineered transport regime exhibits exceptional resilience against strong non-magnetic Anderson disorder and geometric variations. These results establish a synergistic paradigm for non-magnetized spintronics, demonstrating how the active integration of spin-orbit coupling and unconventional magnetism can yield disorder-resilient spintronic logic.
summary: The paper addresses the challenge of maintaining spin coherence in advanced magnetic materials by proposing an active compensation scheme. By tuning gate-induced Rashba SOC to exactly balance the material's intrinsic $p$-wave exchange coupling, the authors restore a generalized SU(2) symmetry. This engineered protection yields robust, electrically tunable Persistent Spin Helix states suitable for high-contrast spintronic switching.
main_problem: The challenge is realizing functional spin-logic devices using unconventional magnets while maintaining long-range spin coherence against scattering and dephasing.
main_result: By engineering a generalized SU(2) symmetry through the active compensation of intrinsic exchange coupling with gate-tunable Rashba SOC, the authors demonstrate robust, electrically tunable spintronic switching.
method: The work uses continuum Hamiltonian modeling combined with Non-Equilibrium Green's Function (NEGF) transport simulations to analyze spin dynamics and conductance.
model_or_system: The system is a 3D p-wave magnet modeled as a spin-FET, described by a Hamiltonian combining $p$-wave exchange ($H_M$) and gate-induced Rashba SOC ($H_{SOC}$).
key_observables: High-visibility Datta-Das conductance oscillations (transport) and the spatially evolving local spin expectation value $\langle oldsymbol{\sigma}(\mathbf{r})angle$ (spin texture).
important_parameters: The critical resonance condition is $\alpha = J \sin( eta - 	heta)$, which tunes the system into a symmetry-protected Persistent Spin Helix (PSH) regime.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes that the spin quantization axis can be constrained to the 2D plane for simplicity in modeling the compensation mechanism.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the comparison of spin texture when the system is at resonance versus detuned, and map out the parameter space $(\alpha/J,  eta)$ showing the boundary condition required for zero conductance (OFF-state).
paper_structure: The paper progresses from identifying the coherence challenge in 3D magnets to proposing active symmetry restoration via SOC compensation. It analyzes the resulting PSH state and culminates by demonstrating its use in a high-contrast, disorder-resilient spin-FET switch.
why_interesting: This work provides a concrete mechanism for engineering topological protection (via symmetry) into solid-state spintronics devices, offering a pathway to robust quantum control elements that are tunable electrically.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

A longitudinal bias V drives the charge trans- port, while a gate voltage VG tunes the induced Rashba SOC (α) to actively compensate the intrinsic momentum- dependent exchange (J), controlling transport between fer- romagnetic leads. (b) Quasi-1D ribbon band dispersions showing perfect spin-momentum decoupling in the general- ized SU(2) symmetric regime (α = J/ √

FIG. 2. Symmetry-protected macroscopic conductance oscillations. (a,b) Ballistic conductance G vs. normalized channel length Lx/lα PSH, demonstrating strict inverse scaling with the gate-controlled Rashba SOC α. (c,d) G under varying non- magnetic Anderson disorder W (100-sample ensemble average), confirming the exceptional immunization of spin coherence against momentum scattering even in strongly disordered regimes. (e,f) Stability of G oscillations against incident Fermi energy E variations. (g,h) Conductance scaling with transverse width Ly; the invariant spatial period confirms the effectively 1D nature of the protected PSH mode. Fixed parameters: m = 0.5, hz = −1.5, J = 0.1 √

FIG. 3. Transport phase diagrams and stringent boundary conditions for symmetry-protected spin logic. (a) Map of G in the (α/J, β) space for injected spin azimuth θ = 0. The generalized SU(2) compensation manifests as a distinct G = 0 contour. (b) Parameterization of terminal N´eel vectors nL/R via polar (θL/R) and azimuthal (ϕL/R) angles. A perfect minimum (infinite ON/OFF ratio) strictly requires antiparallel polarizer and analyzer alignments. (c,d) Conductance maps navigating the FM N´eel angles: (c) enforcing |θL −θR| = π, and (d) locking ϕL + ϕR = π. Solid black curves indicate actively protected G = 0 regions, flawlessly matching analytical resonance conditions (open circles). We set...

FIG. 4. Universal stability of synergistic switching operations. The macroscopic switching profile drops precipitously to an exact OFF-state at the critical compensation α/(J sin β) = 1. This behavior is highly robust against diverse perturbations: (a) varying FM-channel interfacial coupling tc, demonstrating immunity to contact scattering; (b) increasing non-magnetic Anderson disorder W (100-sample average), highlighting the insulation of spin coherence by the generalized SU(2) symmetry; (c) normalized channel length Lx/lα PSH, showing the deep OFF-state requires only commensurability with the PSH period; and (d) transverse width Ly, confirming full geometric scalability. Spintronics.note: The new paper focuses on electrically tunable spintronic switching in magnetic materials using gate-tunable SOC and transport measurements (Datta-Das oscillations), which is a direct extension of quantum-optics concepts applied to magnet-metal heterostructures.
